title: Pluto / StreamlinePro
subtitle: User journeys — what happens when you tap, by role

roles.name: Manager
roles.description: Company-scope admin. Sees Pulse landing, can invite users, edit company settings.

roles.name: Dispatcher
roles.description: Schedules and assigns work. Lives in DispatchScreen and creates bookings.

roles.name: Worker
roles.description: Field installer. Lives in MyAppointments; runs jobs from BookingDetail.

roles.name: Customer
roles.description: End-customer of a booked service. Touches the app only via emailed token links.

screens.name: My Jobs
screens.description: Worker landing — tabs: Dispatched / Paid / Completed / All.

screens.name: Add user (modal)
screens.description: Inline modal — name, email, phone, role dropdown.

screens.name: Out-of-band channel
screens.description: SMS, Slack, voice — wherever the manager passes the temp password.

screens.name: Dispatch
screens.description: Bookings grouped by date/status. Dispatcher's primary surface.

screens.name: New booking
screens.description: Multi-step form: customer → location → details (title, date, time, items, notes).

screens.name: Booking detail
screens.description: Opens from Dispatch or MyAppointments. Status actions live here.

screens.name: Complete booking
screens.description: Checklist items (text + photo upload) + 'Submit' to finalize.

screens.name: Public review page
screens.description: Token-gated. Served by the web bundle behind nginx; no native screen.

journeys.name: Manager: invite + onboard a worker
journeys.description: Manager creates the account from Settings → Team. There is no magic-link email — the API returns a temp password, which the manager passes to the worker out-of-band. The worker then signs in for the first time and lands on My Jobs.

steps.action: Tap the Settings tab in the drawer/tab bar
Tap 'Team' in the settings list
Tap the + button in the header
steps.note: Opens UserFormModal inline — not a new route.

steps.action: Fill first_name, last_name, email, phone, role; tap Save

server.label: POST /api/v1/users
server.note: Manager scope is force-pinned to actor.company_id. Application-role actors may pass company_id.

steps.note: Toast lingers ~6s: 'Created Jane Doe. Temp password: ABCD-1234'. Modal closes; new row appears in Team.

steps.action: Copy the temp password and share it with the new worker (SMS / Slack / voice / etc.)
steps.note: This is the only place the temp password is shown. If the manager loses it, they have to re-issue via the password-reset endpoint.

steps.action: Open the app, type their email + the temp password
Tap 'Sign in'

server.label: POST /api/v1/auth/login
server.returns: { token: sanctum-pat, user: { id, email, role, company_id } }
server.note: Throttled 30/min per IP. Sanctum token stored in AsyncStorage; subsequent requests carry `Authorization: Bearer …`.

steps.note: Landing depends on role: Worker → My Jobs; Manager/installer → Pulse.

journeys.name: Dispatcher: create + dispatch a booking
journeys.description: Dispatcher creates a booking on the multi-step form, then transitions it from 'Created' → 'Dispatched'. That status transition (not the create) is what fires the customer-facing confirmation email.

steps.action: Tap 'New booking' on the Dispatch board
Pick or create the customer (step 1 of the form)
Pick the customer location (step 2)
steps.note: RentCast enrichment runs on the address.

steps.action: Fill title, date, time, items, workers; tap Save

server.label: POST /api/v1/bookings
server.note: BookingService::createForCompany inside a DB transaction. No email/notification yet — that fires on dispatch.

steps.note: Lands back on the Dispatch board; the new booking appears in the 'Created' column.

steps.action: Tap the new booking card to open its detail
Tap 'Dispatch'

server.label: POST /api/v1/bookings/{id}/dispatch
server.note: Gate: billable product + appointment slot + valid address + ≥1 assigned worker. EmailService::sendBookingEmail(booking, 'booking_dispatched') is then called synchronously to the customer.

steps.note: Status badge flips from 'Created' to 'Dispatched'. Customer receives the confirmation email.

journeys.name: Worker: accept a dispatched job → mark complete
journeys.description: Worker sees the dispatched job in the 'Dispatched' tab of My Jobs, opens it, runs through the checklist on Complete Booking, uploads photos, and submits. Once status flips to 'Completed', a background job emails the customer a review link (see customer-review).

steps.action: Tap the 'Dispatched' tab to filter the list
Tap a dispatched job row
Step the status through 'On the way' → 'On site'

server.label: PUT /api/v1/bookings/{id} (status field)
server.note: Same endpoint as edit; status field is allowlisted.

steps.note: Buttons share guards with the Kanban-drag rules — see BookingService::dispatch / completeBooking.

steps.action: Tap 'Complete'
Fill checklist text items + open the image picker for each photo slot
For each photo: pick or capture with expo-image-picker

server.label: POST /api/v1/bookings/upload-photo (multipart)
server.note: PhotoUploadService::storeTemp only writes S3 temp/. No DB write yet — the path is held in mobile state and submitted with the rest of the form.

steps.action: Tap 'Submit'

server.label: POST /api/v1/bookings/{id}/complete
server.note: Booking.status flips to 'Completed' inside a transaction. PhotoUploadService::moveToBooking promotes the temp paths to permanent and writes them into bookings.photos (JSON array).

steps.note: Job disappears from 'Dispatched' tab; reappears in 'Completed'. The status change triggers the review email — see next journey.

journeys.name: Customer: receive review email → submit NPS
journeys.description: Triggered by the worker's 'Submit' in the previous journey. A model observer on Booking spots the status flip to 'Completed' and queues SendReviewRequestJob, which emails the customer a tokenized link.

steps.action: (Triggered by the worker submitting in the previous journey)

server.label: Booking::booted() observer → SendReviewRequestJob::dispatch(bookingId)
server.note: Booking.php:37 — `wasChanged('status') && status === 'Completed'`. Job sends the email via EmailService (SendReviewRequestJob.php:54).

steps.note: Background job — runs on the queue worker, not in-request.

steps.action: See 'How did we do?' email in inbox
Tap the signed link in the email
steps.note: Opens https://app/reviews/{token} in the browser. Served by the web bundle (nginx → /var/www/plutoapp-dist).

steps.action: Page loads with booking summary + the review form
Slide NPS 0–10, optionally pick tags, type a comment
Tap Submit
steps.note: Page shows a thank-you state. The review surfaces internally in the ReviewsScreen for managers.
